value.0: Greetings <Sir/Madam>. How may I help you.
I agree on both conclusions... it is nice and it is empty.
Yes we do.
Uhh... the uhh... musicians are actually sick and they couldn't come today. They should be back one of these days.
We can not find anyone of good enough quality to satisfy the requests of our regular customers.
Not lost, just temporarily uninterested.
*Looks you under the eye* Whatever you say <Sir/Madam>...
Perhaps, but we'll get our customers to visit more often as soon as the problems are sorted out.
I can't say really. From time to time we have more regulars, but lately things are just changing. Something is in the air.
Well... actually... nothing too important to talk about now.
Maybe I should just bring you a drink, and you find someone else to chat to.
I apologise to you, but I am busy serving what little customers we have.
Hah! He would just show you the door.
That is what they all say...
Well... *sigh* Allright. But you must promise to keep it to yourself! *raises eyebrows and gets close to you*
I will just tell you a brief introduction to our problems. You may talk to our chef more, as he can tell you more details.

There is a certain to us unknown power of darkness rising in the east. These are just rumors to many Shire folk, but we have already felt its presence. There is a fellow in a nearby town called Sharkey. He seems to hide more knowledge about this power than most other men.

He is preparing himself to serve this evil, whatever it may be, and gain power for himself over the lands of Shire and local towns such as Bree. He has already started his mission and our Inn seems to be posing some sort of a problem for him and his men... Perhaps from here you will be better off talking to our cook. You can find him in the kitchen.
You know? You are right. I don't. We don't. In fact, since you don't get this quite so seriously may I ask you to let me continue my work... No no no. May i TELL you to let me continue my work.
I would, but I am rather busy. Farewell.
No, we aren't rude, but the matters are rather serious for us and we dislike talking about them to just anyone. Now please let me be.
Hm... *looks worried* yes nothing to worry about, I assure you.
Well, there are a lot of nice things that we can offer you. Would you like something to relax you, to cure you, or perhaps just something to rid you of thirst?
Any drink is what you get then <Sir/Madam>... *He gives you a small bottle to drink*
Here you are <Sir/Madam>, it is on the house.
That would be 10 gp and you are as good as new.
Well, here you go. I believe you will feel more lively once you have it.
Oh, nothing. I'm just looking around your inn. Looks nice, but slightly emptier than I should expect.
Don't you have some music around this time?
Well, where is it now? I'd like to hear some of the Shire music!
Allright. Hope they return soon.
Hmm, can't you get a replacement?
That might pose a problem for you. Because as such, your Inn is really not worth much mention.
You must be worried about losing your job. But I wonder why an Inn with a reputation such as this should lose customers.
Haha. No, your Inn has simply become old-fashioned, dirty and smelly. You don't stand a chance, better find another job soon.
Knowing Inns, that's usually the beginning of a bad end.
Do you have more regulars or just passer-bys?
Hm. Yes I feel it too. You will do fine, no worries.
So you do have problems?
But I'd like to hear it still.
How long have you been working here?
Allright... allright... I'll just sit here then, on my own... alllll alone....
You know what? I'll just leave this rude filthy place, don't want you giving me some rotten juices...
Should I talk to the owner of this place to find out?
Why would he show me the door? I may just be trying to help.
Who is they? Would you please tell me, I am uniquely curious.
Your secret will be safe with me.
OK.
Yes...
Yes, I certainly will.
Oh come on. Like you have a death threatening problem...
OK! Relax...
Listen, you're just getting on my nerves now. Will you tell me what is going on, or not?
Fine!
I see, so you are all rude. No wonder you have no customers.
Allright, suit yourself.
Whatever. Get me a drink then.
If you say so.
OK then. How about you get me a drink?
It is probably emptier because it is this time of day, however. I'm sure you get more customers at other times.
End Dialog
How about a drink?
I'd just like to have something nice to drink.
Oh, can you not just offer me any drink? Don't you realise when you become annoying?
I'm just thirsty.
I believe I need to be cured.
A drink that relaxes? That sounds nice, I'll have that.
